name: Schema migrations + zombie cleanup
description: M1 schema hardening (migration 026: tier, tier_version, ambiguity_class, link_policy, new PK). S4 ontology version pinning (migration 027: source_version, supersedes_id). D3 staging tables + promote_harvest skeleton (migration 028). M3.5.0 query_log table (migration 029). Zombie harvest_runs cleanup script for GCMD/SPASE stuck in 'running' state. All migrations must be idempotent.
acceptance: Migration 026 applied to scix_test: `\d document_entities` shows columns tier SMALLINT NOT NULL, tier_version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1; PK is (bibcode, entity_id, link_type, tier); entities.ambiguity_class enum has values unique/domain_safe/homograph/banned; entities.link_policy enum has values open/context_required/llm_only/banned
Integration test inserts two rows with same (bibcode, entity_id, link_type) but different tier values without collision; DELETE WHERE tier=2 removes exactly one tier's rows
Migration 027 applied: entities.source_version TEXT column exists; entities.supersedes_id BIGINT self-FK exists

Migration 028 applied: entities_staging, entity_aliases_staging, entity_identifiers_staging tables exist with same schema as production plus staging_run_id; promote_harvest(run_id BIGINT) function defined (stub body acceptable if u04 unit completes it)
Migration 029 applied: query_log table exists with columns id BIGSERIAL PK, ts TIMESTAMPTZ, tool TEXT, query TEXT, result_count INT, session_id TEXT NULL, is_test BOOL NOT NULL DEFAULT false

scripts/cleanup_harvest_zombies.py exists and when run against scix_test with a fixture zombie row in harvest_runs(status='running', started_at<now()-interval '6 hours'), marks the row as status='aborted_zombie' and prints a summary
pytest tests/test_migrations.py passes (all migrations apply cleanly on a fresh scix_test DB; re-running is a no-op)

name: Fix silent collision-drop in link_entities.py:70
description: M2. The caching resolver in src/scix/link_entities.py around line 70 does `if key and key not in cache`, silently letting the first ontology own every ambiguous canonical name. Replace with a collision-aware cache that stores all candidates per key and surfaces ambiguity to the disambiguator. Separate commit, touches a single file + one test module.
acceptance: src/scix/link_entities.py no longer drops collisions at the cache layer; the resolver returns all candidate entities for an ambiguous canonical name

tests/test_link_entities_collision.py: a unit test creates two entities with the same lowercase canonical name (e.g. 'mercury' from SSODNet and GCMD), calls the resolver, and asserts len(candidates) >= 2
pytest tests/test_link_entities_collision.py passes with 0 failures
git log shows the fix in its own commit prefixed with `prd-build: u02-collision-bug-fix`

name: Canonical resolve_entities() service (M13, D5)
description: M13 — Build the single canonical Python service src/scix/resolve_entities.py exposing `resolve_entities(bibcode, context) -> EntityLinkSet`. EntityLinkSet is a @dataclass(frozen=True) whose __init__ requires a module-private _ResolverToken sentinel. Service has four internal lanes (static-core read, jit_cache hit, live_jit stub, local_ner stub — lanes may be mock/stub at this stage, real backends come from u10). AST-level lint (libcst) walks src/ and fails if any file outside src/scix/resolve_entities.py writes document_entities or document_entities_jit_cache, or reads document_entities_canonical. Hypothesis property test asserts set-equality across all four mock lanes. Benchmark suite captures per-lane p95 latency.
acceptance: src/scix/resolve_entities.py defines `def resolve_entities(bibcode: str, context: EntityResolveContext) -> EntityLinkSet` and is the sole exported entry point

EntityLinkSet is `@dataclass(frozen=True)`; its __init__ requires a module-private `_ResolverToken` sentinel; a unit test in tests/test_resolve_entities_type_guard.py asserts `EntityLinkSet(entities=frozenset())` raises TypeError when called from outside src/scix/resolve_entities.py

scripts/ast_lint_resolver.py uses libcst to walk src/, detects any write to document_entities / document_entities_jit_cache or read from document_entities_canonical originating outside src/scix/resolve_entities.py, and exits non-zero if found; test asserts a deliberately planted violation fails the lint
Unit tests cover all four internal lanes (static, jit_cache_hit, live_jit, local_ner) with mock backends; pytest tests/test_resolve_entities.py passes

Hypothesis property test in tests/test_resolve_entities_invariant.py with >=100 randomized inputs asserts that for fixed (bibcode, candidate_set_hash, model_version), the returned EntityLinkSet has identical entity-id sets across all four lanes (confidence values may differ by <=0.01)

tests/bench_resolve_entities.py benchmark suite measures per-lane p95 latency and writes to build-artifacts/m13_latency.md; static lane p95 <= 5ms, jit_cache hit p95 <= 25ms budgets are asserted (live_jit and local_ner budgets are asserted against mocks)
All acceptance pytest runs exit with 0 failures; ast_lint_resolver.py exits 0 on current src/

name: Staging + promote_harvest + cost ceilings (D3)
description: Complete the promote_harvest() pg function body and wire the Python wrapper src/scix/harvest_promotion.py. Implements S4 shadow-diff as a blocking gate: >2% canonical shrinkage, >5% alias shrinkage, per-source floors, orphan-prevention (entities with >=1000 document_entities rows cannot disappear), schema mismatch rejection. Also implements D3c LLM cost ceilings (src/scix/llm_cost_ceiling.py): per-day $50 cap via a Redis-backed or Postgres-backed counter keyed by YYYY-MM-DD, per-query $0.01 cap enforced pre-call via token estimation. scripts/replay_harvest.py round-trips a snapshot back into staging.
acceptance: src/scix/harvest_promotion.py exposes `promote_harvest(run_id: int) -> PromotionResult`; PromotionResult is a frozen dataclass with fields accepted: bool, reason: Optional[str], diff: dict
Unit test: a synthetic staging run with >2% canonical shrinkage fails promotion with reason containing 'canonical_shrinkage'; staging data is preserved; harvest_runs.status set to 'rejected_by_diff'
Unit test: a synthetic clean staging run is promoted atomically under pg_try_advisory_lock('entities_promotion'); entities/entity_aliases/entity_identifiers rows appear in production tables
Unit test: a staging run that would orphan an entity with >1000 document_entities rows is rejected
scripts/replay_harvest.py --source <src> --snapshot <date> loads a .jsonl.gz snapshot from data/entities/snapshots/ into staging; test asserts round-tripping produces zero diff

src/scix/llm_cost_ceiling.py exposes `check_and_reserve(estimated_cost_usd: float) -> bool` and `record_actual(actual_cost_usd: float) -> None`; unit test: a call that would exceed the $50 daily cap returns False; a call exceeding the $0.01 per-query cap returns False
pytest tests/test_promote_harvest.py tests/test_llm_cost_ceiling.py tests/test_replay_harvest.py passes

name: Ambiguity class annotation for all entities (M5)
description: M5. Populate entities.ambiguity_class offline. Rules: `banned` for any canonical/alias that matches the top-20K English words at WordFreq Zipf >= 3.0 or is <= 2 characters; `homograph` for any name that collides with another entity's canonical or alias across the full graph; `domain_safe` for entities with >=6-character names unique in the graph and a single ontology of origin; `unique` otherwise. Writes updates in bulk via UPDATE ... FROM VALUES. Ships a 50-random-example spot-check audit per class.
acceptance: scripts/classify_entity_ambiguity.py runs against scix_test, populates entities.ambiguity_class for every row, and emits progress to stdout
src/scix/ambiguity.py exposes `classify(canonical_name: str, aliases: list[str], source_count: int, collision_count: int) -> AmbiguityClass` (pure function, unit tested)

Unit test covers all four classifications including edge cases: 2-character name -> banned; 'the' (Zipf >= 3) -> banned; 'HST' with a collision -> homograph; 'GALFA-HI' unique 8-char single-source -> domain_safe
After running on scix_test fixture data, `SELECT ambiguity_class, count(*) FROM entities GROUP BY 1` returns 4 buckets with non-null counts
build-artifacts/ambiguity_audit.md generated with 50 random spot-check examples per class (200 total), formatted as a markdown table
pytest tests/test_ambiguity.py passes with 0 failures

name: Tier 1 keyword -> entity exact match + audit (M3)
description: M3. Single SQL pass joining papers.keywords against entities.canonical_name + entity_aliases.alias, writing to document_entities with tier=1, link_type='keyword_match', confidence=1.0. Then sample 200 of the resulting links stratified across ontologies and arxiv_class; label them via an LLM-judge scaffold for now (human audit lands in u11). Produces the delta report build-artifacts/tier1_audit.md.
acceptance: scripts/link_tier1.py runs a single SQL pass and writes rows with tier=1, link_type='keyword_match', confidence=1.0 into document_entities

Against a seeded scix_test fixture with >=10 papers and >=20 entities, the script writes >=5 new tier=1 rows (production scale of >=3M is not asserted in test -- the test asserts correctness, not scale)

scripts/audit_tier1.py draws a stratified 200-sample across source + arxiv_class buckets, writes sample to build-artifacts/tier1_audit.md with columns bibcode, entity_id, canonical_name, source, arxiv_class, label_placeholder, and computes a Wilson 95% CI for the precision placeholder
pytest tests/test_tier1.py passes with 0 failures; test includes an end-to-end run against a fixture DB and asserts rows are written and audit markdown is generated

name: query_log bootstrap + curated core selection (M3.5)
description: M3.5.0/1/2. src/scix/query_log.py provides a write API the MCP server calls (structured log -> row in query_log); scripts/backfill_query_log.py replays existing MCP session logs into query_log. scripts/curate_entity_core.py runs the three-pass ranking (zero-result gap candidates -> Wikidata backfill hook -> ambiguity_class='unique' with >=1 query_log hit) and writes build-artifacts/curated_core.csv with exactly <=10,000 rows. core_lifecycle.py implements the promotion/demotion trigger with a core_promotion_log table.
acceptance: src/scix/query_log.py exposes `log_query(tool: str, query: str, result_count: int, session_id: Optional[str]=None, is_test: bool=False) -> None`; unit test asserts a row is written with ts~=now()
scripts/backfill_query_log.py takes a --source <path> argument and replays structured MCP session logs into query_log; test asserts >=N rows inserted from a fixture log file

scripts/curate_entity_core.py runs a three-pass ranking against scix_test fixture data and produces build-artifacts/curated_core.csv with columns entity_id, canonical_name, source, ambiguity_class, pass_triggered, query_hits_14d, zero_result_hits_14d; the row count is <= 10,000 (hard-enforced)

src/scix/core_lifecycle.py exposes `promote(entity_id: int) -> None` and `demote(entity_id: int) -> None`; a `core_promotion_log` table is created (migration or inline DDL); unit test asserts that promoting an 10,001st entity auto-demotes the lowest-query-hit entity
build-artifacts/curated_core_stratification.md generated with per-source counts showing at least 6 of 7 live harvesters represented in the fixture
pytest tests/test_query_log.py tests/test_curated_core.py passes with 0 failures

name: document_entities_canonical fusion MV (M8)
description: M8. Build materialized view document_entities_canonical that computes fused confidence via noisy-OR: `fused = 1 - exp(SUM(ln(1 - c_t * w_t)))`. tier_weight(tier) is an IMMUTABLE SQL function. Initial placeholder weights: t1=0.98, t2=0.85, t3=0.92 (deprecated slot), t4=0.50, t5=0.88 (JIT). MV has a UNIQUE index on (bibcode, entity_id) required for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY. tier_weight_calibration_log table records each calibration version. Wire a dirty-flag-driven refresh job capped at <=1 refresh/hour. All writes/reads route through M13's resolve_entities (u03).
acceptance: migrations/031_fusion_mv.sql creates document_entities_canonical MV, tier_weight(tier SMALLINT) IMMUTABLE function, tier_weight_calibration_log table, and required indexes (UNIQUE on (bibcode, entity_id), (entity_id, fused_confidence DESC), (bibcode))
REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY document_entities_canonical succeeds on a populated fixture (the UNIQUE index makes it eligible)

src/scix/fusion_mv.py exposes `mark_dirty() -> None` and `refresh_if_due(min_interval_seconds: int = 3600) -> bool`; rate-limits to <=1 refresh/hour via a last_refresh_at row in a fusion_mv_state table

On a seeded fixture with >=5 tiers of rows per (bibcode, entity_id), `SELECT fused_confidence FROM document_entities_canonical WHERE bibcode=X AND entity_id=Y` matches the closed-form `1 - exp(sum(ln(1 - c_t * tier_weight(t))))` to within 1e-9
tier_weight_calibration_log populated with an initial 'placeholder_2026-04-12' row on migration
Query latency test: on a row with >=100 synthetic links, `SELECT * FROM document_entities_canonical WHERE entity_id=$1 ORDER BY fused_confidence DESC LIMIT 20` returns in <100ms
pytest tests/test_fusion_mv.py passes with 0 failures

name: Tier 2 Aho-Corasick + S2 Adeft disambiguators (M6, S2)
description: M6 + S2. Build a pyahocorasick automaton over curated-core entities with ambiguity_class in {unique, domain_safe, homograph}. Homographs fire only when a disambiguating long-form alias is also present in the abstract. Parallel execution via multiprocessing.shared_memory or fork+pickle. Writes tier=2 rows with confidence from a 7-feature context-vector logistic. S2: per-acronym Adeft-style char n-gram TF-IDF + logistic regression for HST, JET, HD, NGC, IC, M31, R, CORE, MAGIC, SAGE, ATLAS, CMS, IRAS, WISE, ROSAT, SOFIA, GAIA, ALMA, MARS, AI - integrated as a post-filter. All writes route through M13's resolve_entities().
acceptance: src/scix/aho_corasick.py exposes `build_automaton(entities: Iterable[EntityRow]) -> AhocorasickAutomaton` (pickleable) and `link_abstract(abstract: str, automaton, disambiguator=None) -> list[LinkCandidate]`

Homograph entities with a disambiguation requirement only fire when a long-form alias is co-present in the abstract; unit test: 'HST' alone in abstract -> no link; 'HST' + 'Hubble Space Telescope' -> link emitted

src/scix/adeft_disambig.py trains char n-gram TF-IDF + logistic regression per acronym from synthetic long-form positive examples; exposes `load_classifier(acronym: str) -> AdeftClassifier`; unit test asserts classifier achieves >= 90% accuracy on a held-out synthetic test set for at least 3 of the 20 acronyms

scripts/link_tier2.py runs end-to-end on a seeded scix_test fixture: builds automaton from curated core, links abstracts in parallel, writes tier=2 rows via resolve_entities() (M13); per-entity linkage cap of 25,000 papers enforced (entities over cap demoted to link_policy='llm_only')
pytest tests/test_tier2.py tests/test_adeft.py passes with 0 failures
All tier=2 writes go through src/scix/resolve_entities.py (u03's AST lint passes on this branch)

name: JIT lane: bulkhead + cache + local NER (M11a/b/c)
description: M11a: async bulkhead with 400ms hard budget, per-tool asyncio.Semaphore, dedicated executor pool, degrade-to-static-core on timeout/vendor error. M11b: persisted document_entities_jit_cache partitioned table (tier=5) keyed by (bibcode, candidate_set_hash, model_version), TTL 14 days via daily cleanup cron. Fire-and-forget writeback via bounded queue (depth 1024); 2 consecutive queue drops page on-call. Bulkhead-degraded responses are NOT cached. M11c: SciBERT/INDUS-NER local inference wrapper (can be a stub for CPU tests; real RTX 5090 deployment happens outside this unit), continuous 5% canary share, overflow routing (bulkhead-degrade -> local NER -> static core last-line). All lanes plug into u03's resolve_entities() via the jit_cache_hit, live_jit, local_ner internal lanes.
acceptance: migrations/032_jit_cache.sql creates document_entities_jit_cache partitioned table with tier=5 default, schema matching document_entities plus candidate_set_hash TEXT, model_version TEXT, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ columns; partition by RANGE on expires_at

src/scix/jit/bulkhead.py: async context manager with asyncio.Semaphore(default=4), 400ms wall budget; on timeout raises BulkheadDegraded; unit test asserts: under forced 2.5s Anthropic latency, bulkhead returns degraded within 410ms; under forced vendor error, returns degraded

src/scix/jit/cache.py: get(bibcode, candidate_set_hash, model_version) returns cached EntityLinkSet or None; put() is fire-and-forget via asyncio.Queue(maxsize=1024); TTL cleanup via scripts/jit_cache_cleanup.py; unit test asserts queue saturation drops new puts and after 2 consecutive drops a pager alert is emitted

src/scix/jit/local_ner.py: wrapper exposing `run_local_ner(text: str, candidate_set: list[int]) -> EntityLinkSet`; may use a CPU stub implementation for tests (real GPU deployment is out of scope); unit test asserts p95 latency <= 275ms on stub path

src/scix/jit/router.py: routing policy - Haiku primary with 5% local-NER canary share (enforced via weighted random), overflow routes bulkhead-degrade -> local NER -> static-core fallback; unit test asserts under forced Haiku outage, routing falls back to local NER (not static-core); under forced BOTH outage, routing falls back to static-core
Bulkhead-degraded responses are explicitly NOT written to the cache; unit test forces a degraded mode and asserts document_entities_jit_cache row count is unchanged
All cache writes and live_jit calls happen only through u03's resolve_entities(); AST lint (u03) passes on this branch
pytest tests/test_jit_bulkhead.py tests/test_jit_cache.py tests/test_jit_local_ner.py tests/test_jit_router.py passes with 0 failures

name: Eval harness: audit table + LLM-judge (M9)
description: M9. Create entity_link_audits table via migration 033, keyed by (tier, bibcode, entity_id, annotator, label, note). scripts/run_audit.py samples 125 examples per tier (500 total) stratified and lets a human annotate via a CLI. src/scix/eval/llm_judge.py runs Claude Opus over 10K random links as LLM-judge and calibrates Cohen's kappa against the human-labeled 500 set - fails the gate if kappa < 0.6. Publishes build-artifacts/eval_report.md with per-tier Wilson CIs.
acceptance: migrations/033_entity_link_audits.sql creates entity_link_audits(tier SMALLINT, bibcode TEXT, entity_id BIGINT, annotator TEXT, label TEXT CHECK (label IN ('correct','incorrect','ambiguous')), note TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (tier, bibcode, entity_id, annotator))
src/scix/eval/audit.py exposes `sample_stratified(n_per_tier: int = 125) -> list[AuditCandidate]`

src/scix/eval/llm_judge.py exposes `judge(links: list[LinkRow]) -> list[JudgeLabel]` and `cohens_kappa(human: list[Label], judge: list[Label]) -> float`; unit test with synthetic labels asserts kappa computation is correct
scripts/run_audit.py --fixture runs end-to-end against scix_test and writes build-artifacts/eval_report.md with Wilson 95% CIs per tier
Wilson CI computation is unit-tested against a known-value case (e.g. 95/100 -> [0.887, 0.978])
pytest tests/test_audit.py tests/test_llm_judge.py passes with 0 failures

name: M4 three-way eval + M4.5 lane consistency
description: M4: on the same 50-query set + >=20 agent graph-walk tasks, compare (a) hybrid baseline, (b) hybrid + static-core filter, (c) hybrid + JIT enrichment. Report nDCG@10, Recall@20, MRR deltas per configuration to build-artifacts/m4_inhouse_eval.md. M4.5: for the same 50 bibcodes, compute per-bibcode Jaccard over entity-id sets from (citation_chain, hybrid_search[enrich_entities=True], SELECT FROM document_entities_canonical). Subtract lane_delta_set (Wikidata-backfill structurally-unreachable entities) from both numerator and denominator. Gate at 5% at the 90th-percentile of adjusted Jaccard divergence.
acceptance: scripts/eval_three_way.py runs against scix_test fixture with 50 seeded queries + 20 graph-walk tasks, outputs build-artifacts/m4_inhouse_eval.md with nDCG@10, Recall@20, MRR for each of the three configurations
In-house-authored disclaimer printed at top of build-artifacts/m4_inhouse_eval.md

scripts/eval_lane_consistency.py computes per-bibcode Jaccard for 50 bibcodes across the three lanes; writes build-artifacts/m45_consistency.md with raw Jaccard, adjusted Jaccard (lane_delta_set subtracted), aggregate distribution, and divergence breakdown by lane pair
build-artifacts/m45_lane_delta.md exists with one row per structurally-unreachable entity and the reason
Gate: 90th-percentile of adjusted per-bibcode Jaccard divergence is computed and the gate status (pass/fail at 5%) is printed; test asserts computation correctness on a fixture with known divergence
pytest tests/test_m4_eval.py tests/test_m45_lane_consistency.py passes with 0 failures

name: Incremental daily-sync integration + circuit breaker (M10)
description: M10. scripts/link_incremental.py runs Tier 1 + Tier 2 inline on new papers from the daily-sync watermark. Loads pre-built automaton from data/entities/ac_automaton.pkl. Circuit breaker: if linkage exceeds 5 min budget, completes sync without entity links and schedules a catch-up job. link_runs watermark table tracks progress. Circuit breaker paged after 2 consecutive trips; watermark-staleness alert when now()-max_entry_date > 24h. All writes route through u03's resolve_entities().
acceptance: migrations/034_link_runs_watermark.sql creates link_runs(run_id BIGSERIAL PK, max_entry_date TIMESTAMPTZ, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), rows_linked INT)
scripts/link_incremental.py runs an incremental linker from the last watermark; test: 1000-paper fixture incremental run completes in <60s; writes tier=1 and tier=2 rows via resolve_entities()

src/scix/circuit_breaker.py exposes a CircuitBreaker class with budget_seconds and open/half-open/closed states; unit test: forced 10-minute delay trips the breaker and the sync completes without links; watermark advances
Catch-up job `scripts/link_catchup.py` runs and backfills the missed papers; test asserts all skipped papers now have links
Alerting: 2 consecutive circuit-breaker trips write a row to alerts table with severity='page'; watermark-staleness check fires alert under forced stale watermark
pytest tests/test_incremental_sync.py tests/test_circuit_breaker.py passes with 0 failures

name: Should-haves: S1 + S3 + S5 + M12 docs
description: S1: compute citation_consistency signal per (bibcode, entity_id) - fraction of outbound citations that also link to the same entity. Add column document_entities.citation_consistency. S3: MCP-internal helper src/scix/query_expansion.py mapping query string -> entity IDs via NN on entity-description embeddings (90K vectors indexed once). S5: migration 035 entity_link_disputes table, src/scix/feedback.py exposing report_incorrect_link() API. M12 docs: docs/mcp_dual_lane_contract.md describing which MCP tools consume the static lane and which consume JIT.
acceptance: migrations/035_citation_consistency_and_disputes.sql adds document_entities.citation_consistency REAL column and creates entity_link_disputes(id BIGSERIAL PK, bibcode TEXT, entity_id BIGINT, reason TEXT, reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), tier SMALLINT)

src/scix/citation_consistency.py computes the fraction of outbound citations that link to the same entity; script populates the column in batch; unit test on a 5-paper fixture asserts correctness vs closed-form
src/scix/query_expansion.py exposes `expand(query: str, k: int = 5) -> list[int]`; test asserts latency < 20ms on a 100-vector fixture index and top-k ranking is deterministic for identical input
src/scix/feedback.py exposes `report_incorrect_link(bibcode: str, entity_id: int, reason: str, tier: int) -> None` and writes a row to entity_link_disputes; unit test asserts row written

docs/mcp_dual_lane_contract.md exists and documents: static-lane consumers (citation_chain, co_citation_analysis, bibliographic_coupling, community_detection), JIT-lane consumers (hybrid_search[enrich_entities=True], vector_search, bm25_search), and the M13 contract
pytest tests/test_citation_consistency.py tests/test_query_expansion.py tests/test_feedback.py passes with 0 failures
